questions.question: A project manager is assigned to a 12-month project that has a $100,000 budget. 6 months have passed and $60,000 has been spent. On closer inspection, the project manager finds out that so far only 40% of the work has been completed. What is the value of the completed work referred to as?
questions.explanation: The value of completed work is definitionally known as earned value (EV) in earned value management. This is a fundamental concept in project management metrics.

questions.question: A project manager is carrying out initial planning activities for a gas pipeline construction project. When estimating the activity of installing 1,000 m of pipes, one team member states that they can run the same length of pipe in 14 hours on a previous similar project. Another team member claims that according to current project conditions, it is possible to run 100 m of gas pipes per hour. Using the analogous estimating technique, how many hours will it take the team to run the gas pipelines?
questions.explanation: Analogous estimation uses historical data from similar past projects to estimate current project activities. The first team member's statement of 14 hours from a previous similar project is the analogous estimate. The 100 m per hour approach would be parametric estimation, not analogous estimation.

questions.question: A project manager is leading a construction project of a shopping center. During the execution phase, the project manager received an approved change request from the client to modify the partition of the center. After checking the change request, the project manager realized that the budget allocated for its implementation might not be sufficient. What should the project manager do next?
options: A. Discuss the budget of the change with the client
B. Implement the change since it is already approved
C. Reject the change request due to its inadequate budget
D. Disregard the change request since the project follows a predictive approach
questions.explanation: Although the change request is approved, the project manager discovered a critical issue—insufficient budget allocation. The appropriate action is to discuss this impact with the client before implementation. The project manager cannot unilaterally reject or disregard an approved change request, as these are not within their authority. The predictive approach does not prevent change requests or discussion of their impacts.

questions.question: A project manager decides to obtain her PMP certification in two months. She creates an exam preparation plan: weeks one to four watch preparation course, weeks five and six take mock practice tests, week seven time off, and week eight sit for the exam. However, in week five, the project manager had to attend a 7-day international conference. To make up for lost time, she cancelled her week off and worked on mock tests during weeks six and seven instead of weeks five and six. This is an example of what?
questions.explanation: A workaround is an unplanned response to a problem or event that occurs during project execution. The international conference was an unplanned incident, and the project manager's decision to cancel her time off and reschedule mock tests to recover the lost time is a workaround—a response to an issue that was not previously identified or mitigated.

questions.question: During project execution, a team member reaches out to the project manager to inquire about the work they need to accomplish in this phase of the project. Which of these documents comprises thorough descriptions of work packages?
questions.explanation: The WBS dictionary is the artifact that includes thorough, detailed descriptions of work packages. The WBS itself shows only the hierarchical structure and names. The activity list contains names of activities but not detailed work package descriptions. The scope management plan is a process document that defines how to manage scope, not detailed work package descriptions.
